meta.setting: Pine pass and long trail out of French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in travel, camp craft, and animal handling

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Bear sign and camp-food pressure in forest travel.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional road, logging, ranching, and mining travel context.

image_prompt.subject: a small line of trailhands and deputy rangers riding through a pine pass with a pack mule, spare horse, and storm weather behind them
image_prompt.scene: late afternoon; rough trail, deer sign in damp dirt, bedrolls, rifle scabbards, a mule shifting its load, and no heroic charge
image_prompt.composition: square trail procession with riders staggered naturally into depth, landscape larger than them, and the pack mule as the practical center
image_prompt.camera: three-quarter rear trail camera like open-world travel gameplay, low enough to read trail objects
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make them useful before they are dramatic; their heroism is arriving with dry matches.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine travel scene
render_traits: slightly simplified rider, horse, mule, and pine silhouettes
modest texture resolution on leather, canvas, wool, stone, and bark
hard ambient occlusion under animals, packs, bedrolls, and trail rocks
late afternoon light with storm shadow behind
believable 1899 trail gear and riding clothes
working-travel composition rather than posed ranger poster
style_modifiers.palette: pine green, trail umber, storm gray, saddle brown, canvas tan, wool blue-gray, mule dark brown, and low sun gold.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, maps, labels, brands, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
cropped pack mule or cropped riders

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The trailhands, animals, pack load, trail, deer sign, and storm weather are readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 trail travel.
The image shows practical travel capability rather than a heroic lineup.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
